title: Reproducible pipeline deterministic AI draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, evidence-boundary fill-gap, and deterministic offline Python lab for Reproducible pipeline; all items remain blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Reproducible pipeline: reject the overclaim
items.prompt: Which statement is source-bounded and corrects this AI misconception? Setting random_state once is sufficient to reproduce any ML experiment on another machine.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Choose the statement that names mechanism, evidence conditions, and the boundary of the claim rather than a fluent or benchmark-based overgeneralization.

feedback.correct: Correct. Version code, environment, data, split, configuration, and artifacts, record all controllable seeds, and report remaining platform or algorithmic nondeterminism.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the overclaim. Version code, environment, data, split, configuration, and artifacts, record all controllable seeds, and report remaining platform or algorithmic nondeterminism.

criteria.description: Selects the source-bounded correction and rejects the documented AI overclaim.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against cited sources and ensure the distractor does not normalize unsafe or overstated claims.

options.text: Version code, environment, data, split, configuration, and artifacts, record all controllable seeds, and report remaining platform or algorithmic nondeterminism.
Setting random_state once is sufficient to reproduce any ML experiment on another machine.

answer.explanation: Version code, environment, data, split, configuration, and artifacts, record all controllable seeds, and report remaining platform or algorithmic nondeterminism.

items.title: Reproducible pipeline: canonical evidence boundary
items.prompt: Complete the exact source-bounded correction phrase; the complete sentence must remain an honest beginner-to-intermediate claim.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Retrieve the short checkpoint from the correction and ensure it narrows rather than expands the evidence claim.

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'data, split, configuration'. Version code, environment, data, split, configuration, and artifacts, record all controllable seeds, and report remaining platform or algorithmic nondeterminism.
feedback.incorrect: The phrase must preserve the technical and responsible-AI boundary. Version code, environment, data, split, configuration, and artifacts, record all controllable seeds, and report remaining platform or algorithmic nondeterminism.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact canonical phrase that completes the bounded source-aligned correction.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the accepted phrase and technically equivalent variants before learner use.
items.template: Version code, environment, [[checkpoint]], and artifacts, record all controllable seeds, and report remaining platform or algorithmic nondeterminism.

answer.explanation: Version code, environment, data, split, configuration, and artifacts, record all controllable seeds, and report remaining platform or algorithmic nondeterminism.

items.title: Reproducible pipeline: deterministic offline Python lab
items.prompt: Read an integer seed followed by item ids. Use a documented local linear-congruential shuffle and print an 80/20 train|test split manifest.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Implement only the stated offline contract, run the visible boundary, and do not infer a real model, safety, fairness, or deployment claim from this toy result.

feedback.correct: The deterministic offline reference checks match the bounded contract; this does not establish real-world AI quality or safety.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check differs; inspect parsing, algorithm assumptions, boundary behavior, and exact output before retrying.

criteria.description: Passes all visible and hidden deterministic offline tests while preserving the stated evidence and non-claim boundary.

privateAuthorNotes: Offline stdlib-only task. Human reviewer must verify algorithm, fixtures, overclaim boundary, and educational validity before any exam use.

answer.referenceSolution:
import sys

def solve(data: str) -> str:
    tokens = data.split(); state = int(tokens[0]) & 0x7fffffff; items = sorted(tokens[1:])
    for index in range(len(items)-1, 0, -1):
        state = (1103515245 * state + 12345) & 0x7fffffff
        selected = state % (index + 1)
        items[index], items[selected] = items[selected], items[index]
    test_count = max(1, len(items)//5) if items else 0
    train = items[test_count:]; test = items[:test_count]
    return f"{' '.join(train)}|{' '.join(test)}"

if __name__ == "__main__":
    print(solve(sys.stdin.read()), end="")
answer.explanation: The exact algorithm, seed, sorted input ids, and resulting manifest make this toy split reproducible without claiming platform-wide stochastic control.
